channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UP breaks four-game losing run with dominant win over UE behind Olango’s stellar play
item.description: The University of the Philippines (UP) Fighting Maroons snapped their four-game losing streak in the UAAP Season 88 women’s volleyball tournament with a decisive victory over the University of the Eas...

item.title: Akande urges Tinubu to show empathy amid Borno attacks during UK trip

item.title: Kerri Einarson leads Canada into women’s world curling final against Switzerland
item.description: Kerri Einarson’s Canadian women’s curling team from Manitoba’s Gimli Curling Club has advanced to the gold‑medal game at the women’s world curling championship in Calgary after defeating Japan 11‑3 in...

item.title: Transport group calls for nationwide jeepney holiday due to rising fuel prices
item.description: The Alliance of Concerned Transport Organization (ACTO) has announced that it will hold a nationwide transport holiday on Monday, March 27, 2026, to protest the continuing increase in fuel prices. The...

item.title: Tesla Semi Gains Favor Among Test-Driving Truckers Ahead of 2026 Production
item.description: Tesla's electric Semi truck is set to enter mass production at Nevada's Gigafactory starting summer 2026, after years of delays and skepticism. Pilot drivers like Dakota Shearer and Angel Rodriguez ha...

item.title: Philippines to temporarily use Euro-II fuel amid oil supply challenges
item.description: The Philippines has authorized the temporary use of Euro-II compliant fuel as part of efforts to manage the impact of the ongoing Middle East crisis on its oil supply. This decision by the Department ...

item.title: Digital Public Infrastructure: A Key to Africa’s Digital Sovereignty and Development
item.description: Africa is at a crossroads in its digital journey. The question now is whether it will continue consuming foreign technology or shift towards producing its own digital innovations that support sovereig...

item.title: ISWAP retaliates after US December airstrike in northeastern Nigeria
item.description: In northeastern Nigeria, the Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) has intensified operations following a United States airstrike carried out in December 2025 that targeted its leadership. In the...

item.title: Meningitis B outbreak in Kent sees cases fall as vaccinations and antibiotics continue
item.description: The number of meningitis B cases linked to an outbreak in Kent has declined after further testing led to the reclassification of several infections. According to the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA),...

item.title: Why Nairobi keeps flooding despite early warnings and heavy rains
item.description: The video explains how Nairobi has been repeatedly hit by severe floods that have taken many lives and destroyed property, yet the city still seems unprepared every season. The report shows that over ...

item.title: Dermot O'Neill supports wife Patricia after her terminal cancer diagnosis
item.description: Dermot O'Neill, best known for his role as Granddad in the BBC comedy 'Mrs Brown's Boys', has revealed that his wife, Patricia 'Chickie' Maguire, has been diagnosed with terminal stage four cancer. Th...

item.title: Punjab Minister Accused of Harassment and Intimidation in PWC Officer’s Suicide Case
item.description: The alleged suicide of Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, a district manager at the Punjab Warehousing Corporation (PWC), has sparked political controversy and calls for justice in Punjab. Randhawa reportedly ...

item.title: Dwight Yorke's Tumultuous Coaching Career: From 'Pub Team' Fallout to Leaving Trinidad and Tobago
item.description: Dwight Yorke, a Manchester United legend and former Premier League striker, has had a colourful coaching career, marked by several dramatic moments. After retiring from football in 2009, Yorke pursued...

item.title: Phivolcs raises Alert Level of Bulusan Volcano after hundreds of volcanic quakes
item.description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) raised Bulusan Volcano’s alert level from 0 to 1 on Sunday, March 22, 2026, after recording a sharp increase in volcanic activity. Sin...

item.title: Helicopter crash in Qatar kills 3 Turks and 3 Qataris, including serviceman
item.description: A tragic helicopter crash occurred in Qatar's territorial waters, resulting in the deaths of three Turkish nationals, including a member of the Turkish armed forces, and three Qatari servicemen. The h...

item.title: PM Modi breaks record for longest-serving head of government in India
item.description: On March 22, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi achieved a historic milestone by surpassing the record for the longest-serving head of government in India. With 8,931 days in office, he has outdone th...

item.title: Arsenal and Manchester City Set to Clash in Carabao Cup Final at Wembley
item.description: On Sunday evening, Arsenal and Manchester City will lock horns at Wembley Stadium for the Carabao Cup final. Arsenal made it to the final by overcoming Chelsea in the semi-finals, while Manchester Cit...

item.title: UK Security Concerns Rise as Reports Detail Iranian Missile Capabilities
item.description: Several UK newspapers lead with concerning reports regarding Iran's missile capabilities. The Sunday Telegraph claims Israel has stated that Iranian missiles can now reach London, following an attack ...

item.title: Israeli Military Probes Missile Defense Failure Against Iranian Attack
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) are conducting an investigation into a failed attempt to intercept an Iranian ballistic missile that struck the city of Beit Shemesh. The missile impact resulted in si...

item.title: Women as First Resurrection Witnesses Contrast Modern Church Divisions on Female Preachers
item.description: On Easter Sunday 2026, many U.S. churches will celebrate Jesus' resurrection with readings from Matthew 28:1-10 or John 20:1-18, highlighting women's central role as the first witnesses. In John's Gos...
